NOW OPEN TO R0MESTEADERS

pages.raw_text:
settler will have everything clear in
his own right.


To the average man some ready
money will be very necessary If he is
not t. meet with hardship. In the
first place the fees to be paid to the
land office at Carson, for making the
formal applications and entries,
amount to ab-ut Sl4. Then the set
tier must live for a year before he
can get crop. The land which he
takes up is, at present, only sand and
sagebrush. When he goes upon it it
will be the same, plus ditch with
water in it. II will be up to him to
build a habitation on that land, to
make bitches, to get a plow and an
axe and other farm implements and
seed for the first crop.


He will have to buy a horse and
